headings: h3: The Conservative Party in Wetherby & Easingwold
h3: What areas make up the Wetherby & Easingwold constituency?
pages.text_excerpt: About Us | Wetherby and Easingwold Skip to main content Wetherby and Easingwold Conservatives Main navigation About Us News Sir Alec Shelbrooke, M.P. People Get involved Contact Wetherby and Easingwold Conservatives About Us Tweet The Conservative Party in Wetherby & Easingwold The Conservative Party has active branches in all UK constituencies hosting a variety of social events, campaigning activity and political events. Anybody who lives in the revised constituency can sign up to be a Party Member and take part in events, policy discussions and leadership elections. We campaign on a number of local and national issues, with the aim of improving the lives of those in the area, as well as protecting our valuable environment for future generations. Why not join us, to help our campaigns, take part in our events and participate in your local community? What areas make up the Wetherby & Easingwold constituency? The Wetherby & Easingwold constituency covers 942 square kilometres across North & West Yorkshire. The land use is 92% agriculture, 3% forest or semi-natural land and 4% built-up areas. It is estimated that Wetherby & Easingwold contributes £2.2 billion to the gross value of the UK economy. The population of Wetherby & Easingwold is as follows: 17% aged 0-15; 22% aged 16-39; 40-64 aged 35%; 26% aged over 65. The ethnic make up of the constituency is 96% White; 2% Asian; 1% Black; and 1% Mixed. 16% of residents in Wetherby & Easingwold are registered disabled. The constituency included the following council wards/divisions: Easingwold Hillside & Raskelf Huby & Tollerton Boroughbridge Ouseburn Spofforth with Lower Wharfedale & Tockwith Bishop Monkton Appleton Roebuck & Church Fenton Tadcaster Wetherby Harewood Join or Renew Help us take action on local issues and build a better Britain. pages.title: Get involved | Wetherby and Easingwold
pages.text_excerpt: Get involved | Wetherby and Easingwold Skip to main content Wetherby and Easingwold Conservatives Main navigation About Us News Sir Alec Shelbrooke, M.P. People Get involved Contact Wetherby and Easingwold Conservatives Get involved Tweet Membership Options Being a member of the Conservative Party means that you have joined the most open and democratic political party in Britain. After the first three months of your membership, you will be entitled to a vote in Party ballots. You will also have a chance to influence Party policy by letting us know your views. Any person living in the United Kingdom is eligible to join the Conservative Party. Applicants are not required to be registered voters or UK nationals. There is no upper or lower age limit on membership, although children under the age of 15 cannot be enrolled as full voting members. Standard membership is £39 per year. For Members of the Armed Forces it's £25 per year and for those under 26 it's £10. You can sign up online - www.conservatives.com/join Get involved Join Events Get a postal vote Join or Renew Help us take action on local issues and build a better Britain.
